title: Speaking: Navigating Formality
learningGoal: Switch between formal (Aap) and casual (Tum) registers smoothly, using the correct possessive pronouns.
instructions: You are hosting a small gathering. You will speak to an elder guest (formally) and then to your younger cousin (casually). Pay close attention to which possessive pronouns you use.
speakingPrompt: Respond to the prompts, switching between 'Aap/Aapka' and 'Tum/Tumhara' based on who you are talking to.
scenario: A family gathering at your house.

subTasks.prompt: Address the elder guest: 'How is your health?' (Health = Tabiyat [Fem])
Offer the elder guest tea: 'Is this your tea?' (Tea = Chai [Fem])
Now turn to your younger cousin (casual): 'Where is your book?' (Book = Kitaab [Fem])
Tell your cousin about his friend standing far away: 'He is your friend.' (Friend = Dost [Masc])

scenarioContext.targetText: आप घर पर हैं। मेहमान आए हैं।
scenarioContext.transliteration: Aap ghar par hain. Mehman aaye hain.
scenarioContext.baseTranslation: You are at home. Guests have come.

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Yeh aapki chai hai kya?' instead of 'Kya yeh aapki chai hai?' is completely acceptable.

criteria.name: Formality Shift
criteria.description: Must use Aapki for the elder, and Tumhari/Tumhara for the cousin. Failure to shift results in a low score.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Aapki tabiyat kaisi hai? Kya yeh aapki chai hai? Tumhari kitaab kahan hai? Woh tumhara dost hai.

feedbackRules.onPass: Phenomenal! You switched formality levels like a native speaker.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the possessive pronouns. Remember to change from 'Aap' to 'Tum' depending on who you speak to.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Watch your adjective endings. 'Dost' is masculine so it takes 'Tumhara'. 'Kitaab' is feminine so it takes 'Tumhari'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: आपकी तबीयत कैसी है? क्या यह आपकी चाय है? तुम्हारी किताब कहाँ है? वह तुम्हारा दोस्त है।

hintText: Speak slowly. The difference between 'Tumhara' and 'Tumhari' is critical.
commonMistakes: Saying 'Tumhari dost hai' (making friend feminine by accident).
